channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ontario Premier Proposes High-Speed Rail Development Along Hwy. 401
item.description: Ontario Premier has expressed a strong interest in establishing a high-speed rail line along the busy Highway 401 corridor, a proposal aimed at improving transportation and reducing traffic congestion...

item.title: Eight BJD MLAs Cross-Vote for BJP-Backed Candidate in Odisha Rajya Sabha Polls
item.description: In the recent Rajya Sabha elections held in Odisha, eight MLAs of the Biju Janata Dal (BJD) reportedly cross-voted in favour of the BJP-supported independent candidate, Dilip Ray. The elections were c...

item.title: Astronomers Discover Sulfur-Rich Molten Planet with Extreme Surface Temperatures
item.description: Astronomers have identified an extraordinary exoplanet, L 98-59 d, located about 34 light-years away in the constellation Volans. This planet, which is more than 60% larger than Earth but only about 4...

item.title: Australian Shares Gain on Falling Oil Prices Ahead of RBA Rate Decision
item.description: Australian shares rose on Tuesday, buoyed by easing oil prices and optimism in global markets ahead of the Reserve Bank of Australia’s (RBA) interest rate announcement. The S&P/ASX 200 Index increased...

item.title: Westerly system to bring widespread rain across Pakistan from March 17 to 20, says PMD
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a weather advisory predicting widespread rain and thundery spells across the country between March 17 and 20, coinciding with the Eid ul Fitr ho...

item.title: NAFDAC recalls MR.7 Super 700000 capsules over undeclared drugs
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) don issue alert to the public sey dem don recall all batches of MR.7 Super 700000 capsules. Di recall na because analysis show...

item.title: Iraq Explores Kirkuk-Turkey Oil Pipeline Amid Ongoing Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: Amid the ongoing US-Israel conflict with Iran in March 2026, Iraq is seeking alternative oil export routes due to the continued blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. The federal government in Baghdad is e...

item.title: Scottish Parliament to Decide on Legalising Assisted Dying for Terminally Ill Adults
item.description: MSPs in the Scottish Parliament are set to vote on the Assisted Dying for Terminally Ill Adults (Scotland) Bill, which would permit adults aged 18 and over with mental capacity, and who are expected t...

item.title: Springbok Sevens claim 2026 SVNS Series title with New York victory over Fiji
item.description: The South African Blitzboks delivered a determined performance to overcome Fiji 10-7 in the final of the HSBC SVNS tournament in New York, securing their fourth tournament win of the season and the 20...

item.title: Investigating How Animals Respond to Environmental Signals Before Earthquakes
item.description: For centuries, unusual animal behaviour preceding earthquakes has been reported globally, including livestock agitation, wildlife disappearance, and atypical emergence of hibernating species. Historic...

item.title: President Marcos inaugurates climate-resilient classrooms in Bogo City, Cebu
item.description: To support uninterrupted education even during calamities, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., together with Education Secretary Sonny Angara, officially turned over Learning Continuity Space (LCS) facili...

item.title: Drone Collides with Baghdad Hotel in Green Zone, No Casualties
item.description: On 16th March 2026, a drone reportedly collided with the Al-Rasheed Hotel located in Baghdad’s Green Zone, according to Iraq’s Interior Ministry. Fortunately, the incident did not cause any casualties...

item.title: Experts Suggest Strategies to Close Gender Gaps in Rivers State
item.description: For International Women’s Day 2026, Rivers State hosted a programme to address gender inequality, where Sylvia Briggs, CEO of the Women Educators Association of Nigeria (WEAN), proposed actionable sol...

item.title: US Allies Decline Participation in Hormuz Naval Escorts as Trump Voices Frustration
item.description: Several major United States allies, including Germany, Spain, and Italy, have declined President Donald Trump’s appeal to send naval forces to protect commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz,...

item.title: Sundry Markets Organizes Free Medical Camp for Yenagoa Community
item.description: Sundry Markets Limited, wey be the people wey get Marketsquare, Nigeria biggest local grocery retail chain, don show say dem still dey support community matter through their medical outreach program, ...

item.title: Letter criticises Barnaby Joyce for hypocrisy in ABC interview on fuel security and preferential voting
item.description: A letter to the editor in The Canberra Times sharply critiques former Nationals leader and current One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce following his appearance on ABC's Insiders program. The writer describes ...

item.title: Serikali ya Tanzania inawezesha uongozi wa adaptive ili kupitisha uwezo wa kutatua changamoto za usimamizi wa afya
item.description: Serikali ya Tanzania imebaini kuwa uongozi wa adaptive utasaidia kuboresha usimamizi wa huduma za afya na kusimamia changamoto ambazo zinapatikana katika sektor ya afya. Uongozi wa adaptive unahitajik...

item.title: Canadians urge federal government to strengthen public healthcare
item.description: Health advocates across Canada are raising alarms over the growing challenges facing the country’s publicly funded healthcare system. Concerns include long wait times, staffing shortages, and underfun...

item.title: Ndavi Nokeri Launches Podcast Exploring Faith, Finance and Wellness
item.description: Former Miss South Africa Ndavi Nokeri is stepping into the podcasting space with 'The Full Circle Podcast with Ndavi Nokeri,' aimed at inspiring and empowering youth and women. Through this platform, ...

item.title: Chancellor Outlines Strategy for UK Growth: AI, EU Ties and Regional Development
item.description: In her annual Mais lecture, Chancellor Rachel Reeves emphasised that the United Kingdom must embrace artificial intelligence (AI) and strengthen economic ties with the European Union (EU) to stimulate...
